title: China-to-Nigeria BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB 44240-2024 / GB/T 36276-2023 Compared with Nigeria Requirements
rows.target_requirement: Nigeria-side acceptance of a China BESS file depends on the specific import and project route: SONCAP for regulated imported goods, SON or project acceptance of NIS / IEC / approved-equivalent standards, dangerous-goods transport evidence, NERC or state-regulator project requirements, and local fire or electrical safety review. China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 should be treated as technical evidence to be mapped, not as automatic Nigerian approval.
target_standards: NIS / IEC / approved equivalent standards where accepted
NERC, fire-safety, electrical-safety, and transport requirements where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 is a China national standard for lithium batteries and battery packs used in electrical energy storage systems, and GB/T 36276-2023 is a recommended China national standard for lithium-ion batteries for electrical energy storage. These are central China-side references for BESS safety and performance dossiers, but they do not create SONCAP, NERC, customs, transport, or local installation approval in Nigeria.
rows.gap: Build a clause-by-clause crosswalk from GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 to the Nigerian request list. Typical additional evidence includes SONCAP route documents, IEC 62619 or IEC 62933 reports where requested, UN 38.3 test summary, English manuals and warning labels, site commissioning records, and authority-specific approval letters.

mandatory_status.target: China GB evidence is not automatically mandatory or sufficient in Nigeria; Nigerian obligations are mandatory only where SONCAP, SON, NERC, state regulator, customs, transport, fire, insurer, utility, or contract requirements apply
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where applicable in China; GB/T 36276-2023 is recommended and commonly referenced

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB documentation is a useful technical baseline for Nigeria BESS projects, but it must be mapped to SONCAP, IEC / NIS, UN 38.3, NERC, fire-safety, and site-approval evidence before relying on it for Nigerian market entry.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / National Public Service Platform for Standards Information (China)

rows.requirement: Fire-Safety and Electrical Safety Review for Nigerian BESS Installations
rows.target_requirement: Nigeria-side BESS installation review should address electrical safety, fire risk, emergency response, site access, ventilation, separation, thermal-runaway mitigation, signage, and commissioning controls. NERC's current code list includes a Health & Safety Code for the Nigerian Electricity Supply Industry, and project approval may also involve local fire authorities, NEMSA inspection, insurers, utilities, and the project owner. Product-level SONCAP evidence should not be treated as a complete installation fire-safety approval.
target_standards: NERC Health & Safety Code for the Nigerian Electricity Supply Industry, Second Edition (March 2026)
Local fire-authority and project-owner fire-safety review where applicable
NEMSA or electrical installation safety inspection where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, and GB/T 36558-2023 can support fire and operational due diligence, but Nigerian reviewers may still require local installation drawings, emergency procedures, inspection records, and authority-specific approval evidence.
rows.gap: Prepare a site fire-safety file separate from product certification: layout, separation distances, HVAC or ventilation, gas detection where relevant, fire detection and suppression strategy, emergency shutdown logic, thermal-runaway evidence, commissioning test records, and English emergency procedures for the Nigerian site team and responders.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where NERC code, NEMSA inspection, local fire-authority review, insurer, utility, project owner, or site permit requires it; not proven as a blanket whole-unit import certification requirement
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese project approval, GB 44240-2024 scope, and local fire review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] SONCAP is not a substitute for Nigerian BESS installation fire-safety and electrical-safety review. Project teams should build a site-specific safety package before shipment and commissioning.

rows.requirement: NERC Mini-Grid and Storage Project Relevance for Off-Grid and C&I BESS
rows.target_requirement: NERC's 2026 mini-grid framework covers isolated mini-grids up to 5 MW per site and interconnected mini-grids up to 10 MW per site, with registration or permit pathways and environmental and safety compliance expectations. BESS used in Nigerian off-grid, mini-grid, commercial, or industrial projects should be assessed under the project structure: a battery product shipment may need SONCAP, while the site or electricity activity may also need NERC, state electricity regulator, utility, environmental, or safety approvals.
target_standards: Permit pathway for larger mini-grid projects
rows.china_equivalent: China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 are product and technical references, not Nigerian project approvals. They should be included in the technical package but do not replace NERC or state-regulator project compliance, interconnection review, environmental documentation, or customer / site safety obligations.
rows.gap: Separate the import file from the project file. For off-grid and C&I storage, identify whether the BESS is behind-the-meter, part of a mini-grid, interconnected with a distribution licensee, or part of a licensed generation or distribution activity. Then map which regulator, utility, community agreement, environmental process, and safety code applies.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific and mandatory where the electricity activity falls under NERC, state-regulator, mini-grid, interconnection, environmental, or safety approval requirements
mandatory_status.cn: China GB evidence is technical support; Nigerian project permits and approvals are separate

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] For Nigerian off-grid and C&I storage, SONCAP may address import conformity, but NERC or state-regulator project requirements can still control mini-grid, interconnection, environmental, tariff, and safety obligations.

rows.requirement: IEC 62619 / IEC 62933 evidence where accepted by SONCAP, SON, tender, insurer, or project reviewer
rows.target_requirement: SON states that Nigerian standards are approved by SON and that SONCAP compliance procedures may use NIS, ISO, IEC, and other approved standards. For BESS, IEC 62619 evidence for industrial secondary lithium cells and batteries and IEC 62933 electrical energy storage system evidence may be requested or accepted in the SONCAP, tender, insurer, or project-review file. The exporter should confirm whether SON has adopted the relevant IEC edition as an NIS or will accept the IEC report as an approved equivalent for the specific shipment.
target_standards: Nigerian Industrial Standard or approved equivalent where accepted by SON / SONCAP
rows.china_equivalent: China-side GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 evidence should be clause-mapped to the IEC edition requested by SONCAP, the Nigerian buyer, the project owner, or the insurer. A Chinese GB report is useful technical support, but it may not satisfy a request for IEC-format testing or an NIS-adopted IEC standard.
rows.gap: Prepare a standards crosswalk that identifies the exact IEC 62619 and IEC 62933 editions, test-lab accreditation, cell chemistry, battery module configuration, BMS protections, thermal propagation evidence, and system-level controls. Get the SONCAP IAF or project reviewer to confirm whether Chinese GB evidence can be used only as support or as an accepted equivalent.

mandatory_status.target: Usually required only where SONCAP, an NIS adoption, tender, insurer, utility, or project approval calls up the IEC evidence; confirm edition and adoption status before treating it as mandatory
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where applicable in China; GB/T 36276-2023 is recommended and commonly used for BESS battery evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62619 and IEC 62933 should be handled as SONCAP or project evidence unless SON, an NIS adoption, or a binding project instrument makes the exact IEC edition mandatory for the shipment or installation.

rows.requirement: SONCAP / SON Import Conformity for Regulated BESS Goods
rows.target_requirement: SONCAP is Nigeria's pre-shipment verification of conformity process for products imported into Nigeria that are subject to SONCAP. SON states that regulated imports require verification and testing in the country of supply, a Certificate of Conformity from an approved Independent Accredited Firm, and a SONCAP Certificate for Nigerian customs clearance. SON also states that the current regulated scope covers imported goods except listed exclusions, so a BESS shipment should be screened by HS code and product description before assuming exemption or whole-unit mandatory treatment.
target_standards: Product Certificate routes PC1, PC2, PC3
Certificate of Conformity and SONCAP Certificate for regulated goods
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 reports can support the China-side BESS technical dossier, but they do not replace SONCAP route selection, approved-lab testing, Independent Accredited Firm review, or Nigerian customs clearance documents for regulated imports.
rows.gap: The exporter should identify HS codes for battery racks, PCS/inverters, containers, switchgear, cables, and spare parts, then obtain written route advice from the selected SONCAP Independent Accredited Firm. Do not present a Chinese GB certificate as customs clearance evidence unless the SONCAP file explicitly accepts it as supporting test evidence.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for SONCAP-regulated imported goods; whole-unit BESS mandatory scope should be confirmed by HS code, product description, and SON / IAF route advice
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where applicable in China; GB/T 36276-2023 is a recommended GB/T standard commonly used for technical evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat SONCAP as a mandatory import-clearance workflow for regulated BESS goods, but confirm the exact product and HS-code scope before claiming a complete BESS system is mandatorily certified as a whole unit.

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries in a BESS shipment need dangerous-goods transport documentation aligned with the UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, section 38.3, and the transport mode rules used for the shipment. Nigeria-side import conformity does not replace shipment-specific dangerous-goods classification, UN 38.3 test summary, packing instructions, marks, labels, and carrier documentation.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, section 38.3
Mode-specific dangerous-goods rules for air, sea, road, or multimodal transport
rows.china_equivalent: China-side GB battery safety reports and SONCAP documents do not replace UN 38.3 transport evidence. Exporters should keep the UN 38.3 test summary, dangerous-goods declaration where required, MSDS/SDS, packing records, and cell or battery model traceability aligned with the actual shipped configuration.
rows.gap: Check whether batteries are shipped as cells, modules, racks, installed in equipment, packed with equipment, prototypes, damaged units, or returned units. The transport file should match the physical packing and should be reviewed separately from SONCAP market-access documents.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for lithium battery transport where dangerous-goods transport rules apply; separate from SONCAP / SON import conformity
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for export transport where dangerous-goods rules apply; China GB safety evidence is not a substitute for UN 38.3 transport evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] SONCAP and China GB reports do not close the transport gap. UN 38.3 and shipment-specific dangerous-goods documentation should be prepared before dispatch.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
